Full stack road mapHow I turned a time-sensitive take-home assignment into a full-stack financial operations platform, the architecture behind it, the problems I encountered, and what I learned along the way.
Building a full-stack application from scratch is one thing.
Building one that needs to handle authentication, CSV ingestion, database persistence, financial reconciliation, analytics, pagination, AI-powered explanations, deployment, and production concerns is a very different beast.
Recently, I worked on a Revenue Reconciliation Dashboard, a full-stack application designed to help finance and operations teams compare order data against payment data and quickly identify financial discrepancies.
The project started as a take-home assignment.
It ended up becoming a practical lesson in how seemingly simple business workflows become complicated once real data, databases, APIs, AI, and production deployment enter the picture.
In this article, I’ll walk through the entire development process:

• What the application does

• Why revenue reconciliation matters

• The architecture I used
